522	/-- **Push–pull functor `G` — composition law** (contravariant functoriality).
523	For composable morphisms `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁`, `h : Y₃ ⟶ Y₂` of `X`-schemes,
524	`G(h ≫ g) = G(g) ≫ G(h)` (writing `≫` for `Over X`-composition; in the informal
525	`∘`-notation of the blueprint this is `G(g ∘ h) = G(h) ∘ G(g)`). Together with
526	`pushPullMap_id` this assembles `pushPullObj`/`pushPullMap` into a genuine functor
527	`(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. Project-local (`lem:push_pull_comp`). -/
528	lemma pushPullMap_comp (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) (h : Y₃ ⟶ Y₂) :
529	    pushPullMap F (h ≫ g) = pushPullMap F g ≫ pushPullMap F h := by
530	  rw [pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw]
531	  exact rawPushPullMap_comp g.left h.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom Y₃.hom (Over.w g) (Over.w h) F
532	
533	/-- **The push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`**, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`.
534	Assembled from the object brick `pushPullObj`, the morphism brick `pushPullMap`, and
535	the two functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`. Contravariant on `Over X`
536	(hence a covariant functor out of `(Over X)ᵒᵖ`): a morphism `φ : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂` in
537	`(Over X)ᵒᵖ` is `φ.unop : Y₂.unop ⟶ Y₁.unop` in `Over X`, sent to
538	`pushPullMap F φ.unop`. This is the functor the planner calls `G`; composing the
539	geometric Čech backbone `coverCechNerve` with it produces the cosimplicial
540	`O_X`-module nerve `CechNerve`. Project-local (`lem:push_pull_comp` consumer). -/
541	noncomputable def pushPullFunctor (F : X.Modules) : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules where
542	  obj Y := pushPullObj F Y.unop
543	  map φ := pushPullMap F φ.unop
544	  map_id Y := pushPullMap_id F Y.unop
545	  map_comp φ ψ := pushPullMap_comp F φ.unop ψ.unop
546	
547	/-- The geometric Čech backbone, lifted to a **simplicial object in `Over X`**: each
548	fibre power carries its structure map to `X` (the augmentation of `coverCechNerve`),
549	and the face/degeneracy maps are `X`-morphisms by naturality of the augmentation into
550	the constant functor. Project-local; obtained from `coverCechNerve` by
551	`CategoryTheory.Over.lift` applied to the augmentation natural transformation. -/
552	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOver (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : SimplicialObject (Over X) :=
553	  Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom
554	
555	/-- The over-category Čech backbone as an **augmented** simplicial object in `Over X`:
556	`coverCechNerveOver` augmented by the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X`. The
557	augmentation map at each simplicial level is the unique morphism to the terminal object
558	(its underlying scheme map is the level's structure map to `X`), and the augmentation
559	coherence condition holds automatically because the augmentation target is terminal.
560	Project-local. -/
561	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
562	    SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X) :=
563	  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
564	    (Over.mkIdTerminal.from _)
565	    (fun _ _ _ => Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext _ _)
566	
567	/-- The cosimplicial `O_X`-module obtained by post-composing the over-category Čech
568	backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
569	push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
570	of `CechNerve` (before adjoining the augmentation `F`). Project-local. -/
571	noncomputable def cechNerveCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
572	    CosimplicialObject X.Modules :=
573	  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F
574	
575	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
576	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
577	
578	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
579	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
580	object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
581	indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
582	`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
583	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
584	index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
585	on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
586	
587	This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
588	(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
589	unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
590	(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
591	`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
592	transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
593	point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
594	`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
595	are what make this assembly legitimate.
596	
597	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
598	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
599	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
600	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
601	  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
602	    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp
603	
604	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
605	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
606	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
607	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
608	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
609	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
610	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
611	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
612	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
613	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
614	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
615	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
616	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
617	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
618	
619	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
620	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
621	
622	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
623	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
624	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
625	```
626	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
627	```
628	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
629	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
630	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
631	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
632	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
633	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
634	quasi-coherent.
635	
636	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
637	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
638	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
639	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
640	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
641	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
642	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
643	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
644	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
645	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
646	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)
647	
648	/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE;
649	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent` and
650	`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`).
651	
652	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be separated and quasi-compact, `F` a quasi-coherent
653	`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (so, by separatedness,
654	every intersection is affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative Čech
655	complex compute the higher direct images: for every `i ≥ 0` there is a canonical
656	isomorphism of `O_S`-modules
657	```
658	  Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F.
659	```
660	In particular, over an affine base `S = Spec A`, taking global sections gives
661	`Hⁱ(X, F) = Čⁱ(𝒰, F) = H⁰(S, Rⁱ f_* F)` as `A`-modules.
662	
663	We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` and compare against the
664	derived-functor higher direct image `higherDirectImage` wherever the latter is
665	defined (`HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules`).
666	
667	Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the augmented Čech complex is a termwise
668	`f_*`-acyclic resolution of `F` (each term is a product of pushforwards over
669	affine intersections, acyclic by `CechAcyclic.affine` via the P3b bridge), so
670	the P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`) gives
671	`Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`. -/
672	theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
673	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
674	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
675	    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
676	  -- Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the Čech complex is a termwise acyclic
677	  -- resolution of `F` (by `CechAcyclic.affine`), so `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`
678	  -- gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`.
679	  sorry
680	
681	end AlgebraicGeometry

6943	  Here \(H^p(U, -)\) denotes the Ext realization of absolute sheaf cohomology fixed in
6944	  Definition~\ref{def:absolute_cohomology}. The proof is the thin assembly of the sub-lemmas through
6945	  the cover-system encoding. Conditions (1)--(2) of the hypotheses are exactly the faces-in-basis and
6946	  cofinality data of a cover system on a basis, so \(\mathcal{B}\) and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) assemble into
6947	  an instance \(s\) of Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system} with \(s.\mathcal{B} = \mathcal{B}\) and
6948	  \(s.\mathrm{Cov} = \mathrm{Cov}\); condition (3) is exactly the assertion that \(\mathcal{F}\) has
6949	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\) (Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}).
6950	
6951	  The conclusion is then the dimension-shift induction
6952	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} applied to this instance: for every basic open
6953	  \(U \in s.\mathcal{B} = \mathcal{B}\) and every \(p > 0\) one has \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). That
6954	  induction internally embeds \(\mathcal{F}\) into an injective \(\mathcal{I}\), forms the quotient
6955	  \(\mathcal{Q} = \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\), keeps \(\mathcal{Q}\) inside the inductive class via the
6956	  {\v C}ech short exact sequence (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}) and its quotient-stability
6957	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}), and bottoms out at the base case
6958	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}).
6959	
6960	  The argument never uses affine sheaf-cohomology vanishing
6961	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}); its only {\v C}ech-vanishing input is condition (3),
6962	  which on the affine instantiation is supplied by the standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing of
6963	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. Thus the dependency
6964	  \(\text{affine Serre vanishing} \to \text{this lemma}\) is one-directional and the graph is
6965	  acyclic.
6966	\end{proof}

6968	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
6969	
6970	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
6971	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
6972	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
6973	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
6974	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
6975	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.

6977	\begin{lemma}
6978	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
6979	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
6980	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
6981	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}
6982	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
6983	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the

6985	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
6986	  % \begin{equation}
6987	  % \label{equation-extended}
6988	  % 0 \to
6989	  % M \to
6990	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
6991	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
6992	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
6993	  % \ldots
6994	  % \end{equation}
6995	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
6996	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
6997	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
6998	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
6999	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
7000	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7001	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
7002	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
7003	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
7004	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
7005	  \[
7006	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
7007	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
7008	    \qquad
7009	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
7010	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
7011	  \]
7012	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
7013	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
7014	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
7015	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
7016	\end{lemma}
7017	
7018	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
7019	% is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
7020	% Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
7021	% In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
7022	% at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
7023	% There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
7024	% Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. Note that
7025	% $M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$. ...
7026	% Let us define a homotopy ... by the rule
7027	% $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}$"
7028	% (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L80--110).
7029	\begin{proof}
7030	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections, lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}
7031	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7032	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
7033	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is a \emph{local}
7034	  question: the complex is exact if and only if it is exact on every stalk
7035	  (equivalently, after localizing the sections at every prime). It therefore
7036	  suffices to check exactness of the augmented {\v C}ech complex stalkwise.
7037	
7038	  Fix an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) of \(X\) and a prime
7039	  \(\mathfrak{p} \in \operatorname{Spec}(A)\). By the affine tilde isomorphism
7040	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} and \ref{lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}) we have
7041	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U \cong \widetilde{M}\) with \(M = \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\), and the
7042	  sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over the faces are the away-localizations
7043	  \(M_{g_\sigma}\). The localization at \(\mathfrak{p}\) of the augmented {\v C}ech
7044	  complex over \(U\) is thus the localization at \(\mathfrak{p}\) of the extended
7045	  complex of localizations
7046	  \[
7047	    0 \;\to\; M \;\to\; \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \;\to\;
7048	      \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \;\to\; \cdots.
7049	  \]
7050	  Since the \(\{f_i\}\) cover \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\), for the prime
7051	  \(\mathfrak{p}\) there is an index \(i\) with \(f_i \notin \mathfrak{p}\); that is,
7052	  \(f_i\) becomes a \emph{unit} in \(A_{\mathfrak{p}}\). Fixing one such index
7053	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), the standard ``one index is a unit'' contracting homotopy
7054	  \[
7055	    h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \;=\; s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, i_0 \ldots i_p}
7056	  \]
7057	  witnesses that the localized extended complex is homotopic to zero, hence exact in
7058	  every positive degree and at the augmentation. This is precisely the standard-cover
7059	  {\v C}ech vanishing already established at the level of sections in
7060	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
7061	
7062	  As this holds for every prime \(\mathfrak{p}\) — hence on every stalk — the
7063	  augmented {\v C}ech complex is exact on \(X\); that is, the {\v C}ech nerve of
7064	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in
7065	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
7066	\end{proof}

7068	\begin{lemma}
7069	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
7070	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
7071	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
7072	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
7073	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
7074	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
7075	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
7076	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
7077	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
7078	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
7079	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
7080	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
7081	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
7082	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
7083	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
7084	  \[
7085	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
7086	      \;\cong\;
7087	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
7088	  \]
7089	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
7090	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
7091	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
7092	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
7093	\end{lemma}
7094	
7095	\begin{proof}
7096	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
7097	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
7098	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
7099	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
7100	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
7101	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
7102	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
7103	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
7104	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
7105	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
7106	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
7107	  additive homology-preserving functor.
7108	\end{proof}

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the augmented Čech complex on `X`

The lemma `cechAugmented_exact` (`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`) asserts that the
augmented {\v C}ech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`
over an affine open cover `𝒰` is **exact** in `QCoh(X)` — i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve
is a resolution of `F`. This is the *un-pushed* (over `X`, not relative to `f`)
counterpart of `CechComplex`/`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. We record here the
structural ingredients that the exactness statement is phrased against: the
un-augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X` (`cechComplexOnX`), obtained from the {\v C}ech
nerve exactly as `relativeCechComplexOfNerve` does but with the *identity*
pushforward — i.e. directly, with no `f_*`. -/

/-- **The (un-augmented) {\v C}ech cochain complex of `F` on `X`.** This is the
alternating-coface-map complex `C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of the underlying cosimplicial object
of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F` (its augmentation `F` is dropped). It is the
`f = 𝟙`/un-pushed analogue of `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`: the same coherence-free
plumbing without the relative pushforward `f_*`, so it is the complex whose
exactness (after re-adjoining the augmentation `F → C⁰`) is the content of
`cechAugmented_exact`. Project-local. -/

    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=

input.new_string:
/-- **The augmentation point of the {\v C}ech nerve is `F`.** The augmentation
object `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left` of the {\v C}ech nerve is `(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F` (the
push–pull functor applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`), canonically
isomorphic to `F` via the unitors `pushforwardId`/`pullbackId`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def cechNervePointIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
    (CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ≅ F :=
  (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X)).obj F) ≪≫
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).app F

/-- **The augmentation map `ε : F → C⁰` of the {\v C}ech nerve.** Built from the
augmentation natural transformation of the augmented cosimplicial object
`CechNerve 𝒰 F` (the map `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ⟶ C⁰`), pre-composed with the unitor
`cechNervePointIso` identifying the augmentation point with `F`. This is the map
prepended to `cechComplexOnX` to form the augmented {\v C}ech complex. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def cechAugmentation (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
    F ⟶ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X 0 :=
  (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv ≫ (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0)

/-- **The augmentation composes to zero with `d⁰`.** The {\v C}ech augmentation
`ε : F → C⁰` followed by the first {\v C}ech differential `d⁰ : C⁰ → C¹` is zero,
the cochain-complex condition needed to prepend `ε` to `cechComplexOnX`. It is the
cosimplicial augmentation identity: `ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹` (naturality of the
augmentation natural transformation against the two coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`), so the
alternating sum `ε ≫ (δ⁰ - δ¹)` vanishes. Project-local. -/
lemma cechAugmentation_comp_d (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
    cechAugmentation 𝒰 F ≫ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 = 0 := by

      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) 0 := by
    rfl
  rw [hd]
  sorry

input.old_string:
/-- **The augmentation composes to zero with `d⁰`.** The {\v C}ech augmentation
`ε : F → C⁰` followed by the first {\v C}ech differential `d⁰ : C⁰ → C¹` is zero,
the cochain-complex condition needed to prepend `ε` to `cechComplexOnX`. It is the
cosimplicial augmentation identity: `ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹` (naturality of the
augmentation natural transformation against the two coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`), so the
alternating sum `ε ≫ (δ⁰ - δ¹)` vanishes. Project-local. -/
lemma cechAugmentation_comp_d (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
    cechAugmentation 𝒰 F ≫ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 = 0 := by
  rw [cechAugmentation, Category.assoc]
  have hd : (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 =
      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) 0 := by
    rfl
  rw [hd]
  have hnat : ∀ i : Fin 2,
      (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫
        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)).δ i =
      (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 1) := by
    intro i
    have h := (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.naturality (SimplexCategory.δ i)
    simp only [Functor.const_obj_map, Functor.id_map, Category.id_comp] at h
    exact h.symm
  have key : (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫
      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) 0 = 0 := by
    simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD]
    rw [Fin.sum_univ_two, Preadditive.comp_add, Preadditive.comp_zsmul,
      Preadditive.comp_zsmul, hnat 0, hnat 1]
    simp
  rw [key, comp_zero]
input.new_string:
/-- **Augmentation kills the first alternating-coface differential** (abstract form).
For any augmented cosimplicial object `N` in a preadditive category, the augmentation
map `N.hom.app [0] : N.left ⟶ (drop N).obj [0]` followed by the degree-0 alternating
coface differential `objD (drop N) 0 = δ⁰ - δ¹` is zero. This is the cosimplicial
augmentation identity (`ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹`, from naturality of `N.hom` against the two
coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`); stated abstractly so the proof does not unfold any concrete
(heavily whiskered) nerve. Project-local. -/
private lemma augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero
    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C] (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented C) :
    N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫
      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N) 0 = 0 := by
  have hnat : ∀ i : Fin 2,
      N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫ (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N).δ i =
      N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 1) := by
    intro i
    have h := N.hom.naturality (SimplexCategory.δ i)
    simp only [Functor.const_obj_map, Functor.id_obj, Functor.id_map, Category.id_comp] at h
    exact h.symm
  simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD]
  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two, Preadditive.comp_add, Preadditive.comp_zsmul,
    Preadditive.comp_zsmul, hnat 0, hnat 1]
  simp

/-- **The augmentation composes to zero with `d⁰`.** The {\v C}ech augmentation
`ε : F → C⁰` followed by the first {\v C}ech differential `d⁰ : C⁰ → C¹` is zero,
the cochain-complex condition needed to prepend `ε` to `cechComplexOnX`. It is the
cosimplicial augmentation identity: `ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹` (naturality of the
augmentation natural transformation against the two coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`), so the
alternating sum `ε ≫ (δ⁰ - δ¹)` vanishes. Project-local. -/
lemma cechAugmentation_comp_d (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
    cechAugmentation 𝒰 F ≫ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 = 0 := by
  rw [cechAugmentation, Category.assoc]
  have hd : (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 =
      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) 0 := rfl
  rw [hd, augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero, comp_zero]

The root cause: `N.hom.app[0]`'s codomain is `(𝟭).obj N.right` while `objD` uses `drop.obj N` — both reduce to `N.right` but the `+` group instances are pinned to syntactically different types, blocking `comp_add`. Let me normalize everything to `N.right` first.

686	noncomputable def cechAugmentation (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
687	    F ⟶ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X 0 :=
688	  (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv ≫ (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0)
689	
690	/-- **Augmentation kills the first alternating-coface differential** (abstract form).
691	For any augmented cosimplicial object `N` in a preadditive category, the augmentation
692	map `N.hom.app [0] : N.left ⟶ (drop N).obj [0]` followed by the degree-0 alternating
693	coface differential `objD (drop N) 0 = δ⁰ - δ¹` is zero. This is the cosimplicial
694	augmentation identity (`ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹`, from naturality of `N.hom` against the two
695	coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`); stated abstractly so the proof does not unfold any concrete
696	(heavily whiskered) nerve. Project-local. -/
697	private lemma augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero
698	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C] (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented C) :
699	    N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫
700	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
701	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N) 0 = 0 := by
702	  have hnat : ∀ i : Fin 2,
703	      N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫ (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N).δ i =
704	      N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 1) := by
705	    intro i
706	    have h := N.hom.naturality (SimplexCategory.δ i)
707	    simpa using h.symm
708	  simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD]
709	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two]
710	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
711	  sorry

-- Ascribe the augmentation's codomain to the `𝟭`-free form `N.right.obj ⦋0⦌`; this is
  -- the `Comma`'s right functor being `𝟭`, which otherwise pins the composition's middle
  -- object to `(𝟭).obj N.right` and blocks every additive distribution lemma's instance.
  have hnat : ∀ i : Fin 2,
      (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
        N.right.δ i = N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 1) := by
    intro i
    have h := N.hom.naturality (SimplexCategory.δ i)
    simpa using h.symm
  show (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD N.right 0 = 0
  simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD]
  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two]

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean

## Summary
- **Added axiom-clean (6 decls):** `cechComplexOnX`, `cechNervePointIso`, `cechAugmentation`,
  `augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero` (private), `cechAugmentation_comp_d`,
  `cechAugmentedComplex`. All `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
- **Blocked / NOT added:** the blueprint target `cechAugmented_exact` itself (exactness of the
  augmented complex) — its proof requires a *stalkwise-exactness criterion for complexes of
  sheaves of modules*, which is **absent from Mathlib** (verified: no `SheafOfModules.stalk`,
  no "complex exact iff exact on stalks" for `X.Modules`). Building that criterion is a
  separate major mathlib-build (see decomposition below).
- **Sorry count:** before = 1, after = 1 (only the pre-existing protected
  `cech_computes_higherDirectImage:780`, explicitly off-limits this lane — untouched).
- File compiles green (no errors); LSP `lean_verify` confirms each new decl axiom-clean.

## Session summary
This lane is "build `cechAugmented_exact` (the augmented Čech complex is a resolution)".
The planner anticipated I would "FIRST construct the augmented-complex cochain object + its
augmentation `F→C⁰` … THEN prove exactness." I built the entire object + augmentation layer
axiom-clean. The exactness proof (stalk-at-prime) is blocked on missing Mathlib stalk
infrastructure for `SheafOfModules`, documented precisely for the next iteration.

## `cechComplexOnX` (RESOLVED — line ~672)
- **Approach:** un-pushed analogue of `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`:
  `(alternatingCofaceMapComplex X.Modules).obj (Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F))`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. The degree-`p` term is `Cᵖ`; the un-augmented complex
  `C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` whose exactness (after re-adjoining `F`) is the content of the target.

## `cechNervePointIso` (RESOLVED)
- **Approach:** `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left = (𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F` definitionally (confirmed by `rfl`),
  so `pushforwardId`/`pullbackId` unitors give `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ≅ F`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## `cechAugmentation` (RESOLVED)
- **Approach:** `(cechNervePointIso).inv ≫ (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app ⦋0⦌ : F ⟶ (cechComplexOnX).X 0`.
  The codomain `(cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X 0` is defeq the augmentation NatTrans's target (typechecks).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## `augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero` (private) + `cechAugmentation_comp_d` (RESOLVED)
- **Statement:** abstract `N.hom.app ⦋0⦌ ≫ objD (drop N) 0 = 0` for any augmented cosimplicial
  `N` in a preadditive category, then `cechAugmentation_comp_d : ε ≫ d⁰ = 0`.
- **Approach:** cosimplicial-augmentation identity `ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹` from `N.hom.naturality`,
  alternating sum `δ⁰ - δ¹`. Proved abstractly so the heavily-whiskered `CechNerve` never unfolds
  (the naturality on the concrete nerve `whnf`-times-out at 200k heartbeats — confirmed).
- **KEY DEAD-END / GOTCHA (documented for reuse):** the cosimplicial `Augmented = Comma (const) (𝟭)`
  puts `𝟭` on the augmentation's **codomain** functor, so `N.hom.app ⦋0⦌` has codomain
  `(𝟭).obj N.right .obj ⦋0⦌`. This pins the composition's middle object, and **every additive
  distribution lemma fails to match** the goal: `Preadditive.comp_add`, `comp_zsmul`, `comp_neg`,
  even `simp` / `simp only` make NO progress (instances live at the syntactically-different but
  defeq `N.right.obj ⦋0⦌`). `Functor.id_obj` / `dsimp` do NOT fix the hidden `≫` middle-object
  index. **FIX: use `erw` (defeq-matching rewrite) for the distribution step** —
  `erw [Preadditive.comp_add, Preadditive.comp_neg, hnat 0, hnat 1, add_neg_cancel]` closes it;
  likewise the final `f ≫ 0 = 0` needs `exact Limits.comp_zero` (not `rw [comp_zero]`).
  This is the *pre-composition* analogue of Mathlib's simplicial `AlternatingFaceMapComplex.ε`,
  which works with plain `add_comp`/`neg_comp` because there the augmentation is *post-*composed
  (keys on the clean sum), so this `𝟭`-codomain wall is unique to the cosimplicial coaugmentation.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — both axiom-clean.

## `cechAugmentedComplex` (RESOLVED)
- **Approach:** `(cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).augment (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)`
  via `CochainComplex.augment` — the complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` with `.X 0 = F`, `.X (p+1) = Cᵖ`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. **This is the object `cechAugmented_exact` is asserted about.**

## `cechAugmented_exact` (NOT ADDED — genuine missing-infrastructure blocker)
- **Target:** `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is exact (acyclic) — the Čech nerve is a resolution of `F`.
- **Blueprint proof:** stalk-at-prime. Exactness of a sheaf-of-modules complex is local; on an
  affine `U = Spec A`, `F|_U ≅ ~M` (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`), localize the augmented complex at a
  prime `𝔭`, some `fᵢ ∉ 𝔭` is a unit ⟹ contracting homotopy ⟹ exact (= `sectionCech_affine_vanishing`).
- **Blocker (verified):** Mathlib has **no stalk functor for `SheafOfModules`/`X.Modules`** and
  **no "complex of sheaves of modules is exact iff exact on every stalk" criterion**. Searches:
  `loogle SheafOfModules.stalk` → none; `loogle ShortComplex,exact,stalk` → none; leansearch
  "complex of sheaves exact iff exact on stalks" → only `HomologicalComplex.exact_iff_degreewise_exact`
  (degreewise, NOT stalkwise) and `TopCat.Presheaf.mono_iff_stalk_mono` / `isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso`
  (object-level iso/mono via `stalkFunctor`, for presheaves valued in a *fixed* concrete category —
  `X.Modules` value category varies, so not directly applicable).
- **Why not papered:** no sorry per invariant; the proof is genuinely unreachable without building
  the criterion first.

## Decomposition handoff for `cechAugmented_exact` (next iter)
Build these mathlib-build pieces (in order), then `cechAugmented_exact` is a thin assembly:
1. **`X.Modules` complex exact ⇐ exact on underlying abelian sheaves.** The forgetful
   `SheafOfModules.forget`/`toSheaf ⋙ forget₂ … AddCommGrp` is exact and faithful; a `ShortComplex`
   in `X.Modules` is exact iff its image of abelian sheaves is exact. (Check
   `SheafOfModules.toSheaf`/`forget₂` exactness API — partial pieces may exist.)
2. **Abelian-sheaf complex exact iff stalkwise exact.** For `TopCat.Sheaf (AddCommGrp)` the
   `stalkFunctor` is exact and the family is jointly faithful/conservative; assemble an
   "exact iff `∀ x, stalk complex exact`" criterion from `TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor` +
   `exact_iff_degreewise_exact`-style packaging. (This is the big missing piece, ~150–250 LOC.)
3. **Stalk of the augmented Čech complex at a prime = localized extended complex.** On affine
   `U = Spec A`, `F|_U ≅ ~M`; the stalk at `𝔭` of `cechAugmentedComplex` is
   `0 → M_𝔭 → ∏ (M_{f_i})_𝔭 → ⋯`. Reuse `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` (unconditional) +
   the `~M` section-localization API already in `CechAcyclic.lean`/`QcohTildeSections.lean`.
4. **Local exactness = the standard-cover contracting homotopy** (one `f_i` is a unit in `A_𝔭`):
   this is the section-level `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` / `exact_of_isLocalized_span` already
   built (P3). Apply at each `𝔭`, conclude via (1)+(2).

Note: the keystone `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` is unconditional (01I8 done), so step 3 has its main
input ready; the gating work is steps 1–2 (stalkwise-exactness reflection for `X.Modules`).

## Needs blueprint entry
All 5 non-private new decls are `lean_aux` nodes with no blueprint block yet (the planner/dag
agent should add blocks so the 1-to-1 Lean↔blueprint map is restored):
- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechComplexOnX` — un-augmented Čech complex `C⁰→C¹→⋯` on `X`. Proof relies on
  `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` + `CechNerve` (P2, done).
- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechNervePointIso` — `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ≅ F`. Relies on `pushforwardId`/`pullbackId`
  unitors + the defeq `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left = (𝟙X)_*(𝟙X)^*F`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmentation` — `ε : F → C⁰`. Relies on the augmentation NatTrans `(CechNerve).hom`
  + `cechNervePointIso`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmentation_comp_d` — `ε ≫ d⁰ = 0`. Relies on the (private) abstract
  augmentation identity + cosimplicial naturality.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmentedComplex` — augmented complex `0→F→C⁰→⋯`. Relies on `CochainComplex.augment`
  + `cechComplexOnX` + `cechAugmentation` + `cechAugmentation_comp_d`.
  This is the object the blueprint lemma `lem:cech_augmented_resolution` (`cechAugmented_exact`) is about;
  the planner may want to add a `\lean{}` hint pointing the lemma's "form the augmented complex" prose at it,
  and split the lemma into "object (`cechAugmentedComplex`, done) + exactness (pending)".

(Private helper `augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero` — bundle its name into the
`\lean{}` of `cechAugmentation_comp_d`'s block to avoid an `unmatched` node.)

## Why I stopped
**Partial progress — real, but the top theorem is missing-infra-blocked.** Added 6 axiom-clean
declarations (lines ~672–797: `cechComplexOnX`, `cechNervePointIso`, `cechAugmentation`,
`augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero`, `cechAugmentation_comp_d`, `cechAugmentedComplex`),
constituting the full augmented-complex object + augmentation layer the planner asked me to build
first. `cechAugmented_exact` (the exactness) is NOT added: its stalk-at-prime proof needs a
"complex of sheaves of modules exact iff stalkwise exact" criterion that **does not exist in Mathlib**
(verified by loogle/leansearch — no `SheafOfModules.stalk`, no stalkwise-exactness reflection for
`X.Modules`). Building that criterion (steps 1–2 of the decomposition above) is the precise next
mathlib-build leaf; with it, exactness is a thin assembly over the already-unconditional
`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` + P3 `sectionCech_affine_vanishing`. No external-LLM key in env
(per PROGRESS.md) so the informal agent was unavailable; used LSP + Mathlib source instead.
Did not touch the protected `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` / its sorry.
